decisions.title: Approve Artifacts RFC for Canvas evolution
decisions.context: ENG-10663 proposes Artifacts framework - critical architectural decision for Canvas product evolution and Build the Moat pillar. Aligns with embedding deeper into customer workflows.

arguments.for: Enables persistent analysis objects, supports Decision Capture north star, differentiates Canvas from competitors
arguments.against: Additional technical debt, resource allocation away from core search improvements, complexity in data model

decisions.title: Prioritize Timeseries Indexing System vs incremental fixes
decisions.context: ENG-10296 RFC for new indexing system while multiple search bugs exist (ENG-10358, ENG-10597, ENG-10655). Nail the Core vs technical foundation tradeoff.

arguments.for: Sub-second search differentiation (GTM-94), scalability for millions of sensors, supports Sift Edge data volumes
arguments.against: Current UX bugs degrade customer experience, resource constraint on fixing production issues, risky big-bang architecture change

decisions.title: Commit to TRMC IDIQ sole-source strategy
decisions.context: CAGR-24: Test center funding opportunity requiring sole-source positioning. Major revenue unlock but requires strategic commitment and product roadmap alignment.
decisions.blockedBy: Need validation that Sift Edge capabilities meet test-center requirements, unclear product-market fit

arguments.for: Large government contract, validates Build the Moat strategy, recurring revenue stream
arguments.against: May distort product priorities, government sales cycle risk, opportunity cost vs commercial growth

decisions.title: Launch joint marketing program model (Sophia Space/Kepler)
decisions.context: CAGR-7: First structured co-marketing approach. Sets precedent for Embed Deeper strategy through customer evangelism. Complements partnership announcements (DES-172, DES-173).

arguments.for: Customer-driven evidence for sales, establishes repeatable partnership model, low-cost demand gen
arguments.against: Diverts customer from product usage, requires legal/approval overhead, unclear ROI metrics

decisions.title: Ship Python Rules to production
decisions.context: No direct ticket but implied by 2026 strategy doc. Critical Embed Deeper capability - analysts write rules in Python vs clicking. High technical risk, high differentiation.

arguments.for: Power users unlock, 10x decision velocity for advanced customers, competitive moat
arguments.against: Support complexity, security sandboxing concerns, small addressable user base initially

timeAudit.weekSummary: High context-switching week: 11 eng tickets, 4 GTM workstreams, 3 customer-facing deliverables. Video shoot prep (CAGR-60) consuming leadership attention.
alerts: Tomorrow's video shoot requires prep - blocks PM/Design availability

4 critical search UX bugs in production while eng focused on infrastructure RFCs
Security work (ENG-10617, ENG-10647) unscheduled but time-sensitive
